summary_en: FY3/26 annual dividend raised to 20 yen/share (15 yen ordinary + 5 yen commemorative for 50th founding anniversary). Dividend policy revised from stable floor to 40% payout ratio target, effective FY3/27; 50% payout ratio targeted from FY3/28 onward. Minimum threshold: 3.5% of equity annually.
title_en: Notice on Dividend Policy Change and 50th Founding Anniversary Dividend
translation_en_brief:
Izu Shaboten Resort announced on 14 May 2026 a dividend increase and policy revision. The FY3/26 (year ending 31 March 2026) annual dividend will be 20 yen per share, comprising 15 yen ordinary dividend and 5 yen commemorative dividend to mark the company's 50th founding anniversary. Prior guidance was 15 yen annually.

The company has also revised its dividend policy effective FY3/27. The new framework targets a 40% payout ratio as the target benchmark, with a 3.5% equity allocation floor as the minimum safety threshold, replacing the prior policy of a fixed minimum 10 yen per share floor. From FY3/28 onward, the company aims for a 50% payout ratio. The revision reflects management's shift toward more aggressive shareholder returns while maintaining investment in core leisure operations and the growth-oriented Ani-touch business, and preserving current financial structure.
